original_hypothesis.title: yieldFeePercentage at FEE_PRECISION (100%) Causes Division by Zero in liquidate
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The _setYieldFeePercentage function uses a strict greater-than check (>) instead of greater-than-or-equal (>=), allowing yieldFeePercentage to be set to exactly FEE_PRECISION (1e9, representing 100%). In liquidate(), the yield fee calculation performs division by (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage), which becomes division by zero when fee is 100%, permanently bricking the liquidation mechanism.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a boundary condition bug in the validation logic. While it requires the owner to set this value, even an accidental misconfiguration permanently disables the core yield-to-prize conversion mechanism.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (yieldFeePercentage_ > FEE_PRECISION) uses > instead of >=; liquidate line ~462: (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage) = 0 when fee = FEE_PRECISION

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Off-by-one in validation allows 100% fee, causing division by zero
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires onlyOwner to set the fee
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent once set - liquidation permanently broken

operational_questions: Can the owner recover by setting a lower fee percentage?
Does the vault have any other mechanism to convert yield to prizes?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All subsequent liquidation calls revert with division by zero
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Vault stops generating prizes, yield accumulates unused
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Users lose prize eligibility from this vault permanently

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Vault permanently unable to convert yield to prizes
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete halt of prize generation from this vault
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Recoverable if owner sets fee back to < 1e9

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
